breaking_news.title: TeamPCP Executes Trivy Supply Chain Attack Deploying CanisterWorm Wiper
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actors known as TeamPCP compromised the widely used Trivy vulnerability scanner, injecting infostealer malware into official Docker images and GitHub repositories. This supply chain attack escalated rapidly, triggering a self-propagating worm dubbed CanisterWorm that targets Kubernetes clusters and deploys destructive wiper payloads.

The breach defaced 44 Aqua Security repositories and exposed developers to credential theft through compromised CI/CD pipelines. Organizations relying on Trivy for container security must immediately rotate secrets and patch systems to prevent lateral movement and data destruction.

breaking_news.title: Crunchyroll Investigates Breach Exposing 6.8 Million User Accounts
breaking_news.summary:
Popular anime streaming platform Crunchyroll is investigating a significant security incident after hackers claimed to steal personal information for approximately 6.8 million users. The breach reportedly involves sensitive user data, prompting an immediate internal probe to determine the full scope of the compromise.

This incident highlights the ongoing risks facing major entertainment platforms storing vast amounts of consumer data. Affected users may face identity theft risks, requiring heightened vigilance for phishing attempts and unauthorized account access.

breaking_news.title: WorldLeaks Ransomware Group Disrupts Los Angeles Metro Systems
breaking_news.summary:
The WorldLeaks ransomware group successfully breached the Los Angeles Metro system, forcing a shutdown of internal administrative computer systems and disrupting station arrival displays. The attack caused significant operational issues, preventing riders from adding funds to TAP cards online or via support channels.

Local authorities declared emergencies in two Bay Area cities following similar ransomware attacks, indicating a coordinated campaign against critical transit infrastructure. This incident underscores the vulnerability of public transportation networks to cyber extortion and the potential for widespread public disruption.

breaking_news.title: Oracle Patches Critical Unauthenticated RCE in Identity Manager
breaking_news.summary:
Oracle released an emergency patch for CVE-2026-21992, a critical vulnerability in Identity Manager and Web Services Manager enabling unauthenticated remote code execution. The flaw carries a CVSS score of 9.8 and allows attackers to take full control of the system over HTTP without prior authentication.

Security researchers indicate the vulnerability may have already been exploited in the wild, necessitating immediate patching for all affected enterprise environments. Failure to update exposes organizations to severe data compromise and system takeover risks within their identity management infrastructure.

breaking_news.title: North Korean APT WaterPlum Abuses VS Code Tasks for Malware Delivery
breaking_news.summary:
North Korean threat actors tracked as WaterPlum have adopted a new tactic using Microsoft Visual Studio Code auto-run tasks to distribute StoatWaffle malware. This campaign leverages malicious tasks.json files within VS Code projects to execute payloads during development workflows.

The attack targets developers and IT professionals, exploiting trust in popular coding tools to bypass traditional security controls. Organizations must monitor for unauthorized task configurations and validate the integrity of development environments to mitigate this sophisticated supply chain vector.

breaking_news.title: FBI Warns of Russian and Iranian Phishing Campaigns Targeting Signal and WhatsApp
breaking_news.summary:
The FBI issued urgent warnings regarding separate cyber campaigns by Russian and Iranian actors targeting users of messaging platforms like Signal and Telegram. These groups are posing as customer support to hijack high-value accounts belonging to government officials, journalists, and military personnel.

The campaigns aim to conduct espionage and surveillance by gaining access to private communications and contact lists. Defenders are urged to enforce strict multi-factor authentication and verify support interactions to prevent account compromise and data exfiltration.

breaking_news.title: CISA Adds Apple and Craft CMS Flaws to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog
breaking_news.summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency added five critical flaws impacting Apple, Craft CMS, and Laravel Livewire to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. These vulnerabilities include buffer overflows and code injection issues that have been actively exploited in recent attacks.

Federal agencies are ordered to patch these systems by April 3, 2026, to mitigate the risk of compromise. The inclusion of Apple iOS flaws highlights the growing threat landscape surrounding mobile devices and web content management systems in government infrastructure.

breaking_news.title: Hackers Exploit Critical Quest KACE Vulnerability in Active Attacks
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actors are actively exploiting CVE-2025-32975, a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Quest KACE Systems Management Appliance systems. Arctic Wolf observed malicious activity starting in early March targeting unpatched SMA systems exposed to the internet.

The vulnerability allows for remote code execution and poses a significant risk to the education sector and enterprise management infrastructure. Organizations must immediately apply patches or isolate affected systems to prevent unauthorized access and potential network compromise.
